_comment: Exactly MAX_WORKFORCE_CSV_ROWS (15,000, see apps/api/src/routes/workforce.ts -- raised from 10,000 for the 1,000-10,000-worker-per-company scale target, so this cap has real headroom above that stated ceiling) valid, distinct rows, month 2027-02 -- every national_id is checksum-valid and unique (prefix range 2,000,000-2,014,999, disjoint from every other fixture/test in this repo), every dNN cell empty (no exclusions). MAX_WORKFORCE_CSV_ROWS itself is enforced at the ROUTE (POST /import/workforce/:month), not inside WorkforceImportService.importCsv/parseWorkforceCsv -- so this fixture's own service-level expectation is simply 'succeeds fully, right at the documented cap'. It is intended primarily for route-level (supertest) and load-test use, where the row-cap check itself is exercised.
call: WorkforceImportService.importCsv(csvText, month, companyId) OR POST /import/workforce/:month (route-level, where the row cap is actually enforced)

expectRoute.note: at exactly the row cap, the route must accept and enqueue the job, not reject it
